content: Just as the high school English writing framework of reflexion as structured critical reflection on one's own output teaches students to refine arguments by confronting how their ideas mismatch reader expectations, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can borrow this explicit scaffolding to turn vague internal self-correction into step-by-step metacognitive practice similar to a guided writing workshop. This structured linguistic pedagogy of reflexion solves a key pain point of current LLM self-reflection, which often produces inconsistent corrections because it lacks a clear, sequential framework for articulating gaps between intended output and received meaning.
